title: DDL Prompt Enhancement for Improved Code Generation
description:
Revise the prompt sent to the OpenAI API for DDL generation to produce more accurate and database-specific scripts. The new prompt structure uses a parameterized format:

- Database type (e.g., PostgreSQL, Snowflake)
- Table name
- Case sensitivity flag for field names
- Special character preservation flag
- Fields and data types in semicolon-delimited format

API parameters updated: Temperature set to 0 (deterministic output), Top P set to 1, and max token length increased to 2048 to handle larger objects. These parameters are hardcoded in the API request and not exposed in the UI.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt uses the new parameterized format with database type, table name, and field definitions
Fields are sent in semicolon-delimited format
API temperature is set to 0 for deterministic output
Generated DDL is compatible with the specified database type
middleware_changes: Prompt construction logic rewritten to use the new parameterized format. OpenAI API call parameters updated (temperature=0, top_p=1, max_tokens=2048).
api_changes: Outbound OpenAI API call updated with new prompt format and parameters. Later migrated from text-davinci-003 to GPT-3.5 Turbo chat completions endpoint.
implementation_notes: The prompt was initially returning empty responses after the update, which was traced to compatibility issues with the model. The OpenAI documentation for GPT-3.5 Turbo was referenced to resolve the issue. The migration to the chat completions API format was required.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the change was limited to prompt text concatenation. During implementation, the updated prompt returned empty responses. The OpenAI GPT-3.5 Turbo documentation was consulted to resolve the issue. After a few iterations the prompt worked correctly.
